description.en: Ukraine's Easter centerpiece: a round, golden egg bread crowned with dough crosses, braids and rosettes, carried to church in the Easter basket for blessing. Gently sweet, the ornament, not the sugar, does the celebrating.
description.de: Das Herzstück des ukrainischen Osterfests: ein rundes, goldenes Eierbrot mit Kreuzen, Zöpfen und Rosetten aus Teig, das im Osterkorb zur Segnung in die Kirche getragen wird. Nur mild gesüßt, hier feiert der Schmuck, nicht der Zucker.
description.es: El centro de la Pascua ucraniana: un pan redondo y dorado de huevo, coronado con cruces, trenzas y rosetas de masa, que se lleva a bendecir en la cesta pascual. Apenas dulce: aquí celebra el adorno, no el azúcar.
description.fr: Le cœur de Pâques en Ukraine : un pain rond et doré aux œufs, couronné de croix, tresses et rosettes de pâte, porté à l'église dans le panier pascal pour la bénédiction. À peine sucré, c'est le décor qui fait la fête, pas le sucre.

origin.region: Ukraine-wide; also Poland, Slovakia and the diaspora
origin.note: Blessed in the Easter basket on Holy Saturday. Rounder, flatter and far less sweet than Russian kulich, paska's glory is its sculpted dough decoration, not icing.

ingredients.note: optional; some families add saffron instead

yield.shape: tall round loaf in a 23 cm springform, decorated top

keeping.note: Best fresh with butter or cheese; toast day-three slices.

method.title: Wake the yeast
method.summary: Warm the milk to about 43°C. Dissolve a spoonful of the sugar in a third of it, sprinkle the yeast on top and wait 5–10 minutes until it foams.

method.title: Mix and knead
method.summary: Whisk flour, salt and remaining sugar. Work in the eggs and yeast milk, kneading while the rest of the milk goes in. Add the melted butter and knead 7–10 minutes more to a soft, supple dough; fold in raisins if using.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let rise in a warm spot about 1 hour, until doubled.

method.title: Shape and decorate
method.summary: Press half the dough into a deep, greased round pan. From the rest, roll thin ropes and form crosses, braids and spiral rosettes; arrange them over the top and fix with a dab of water.

method.title: Proof
method.summary: Cover and proof about 45 minutes, until almost doubled and the decorations have plumped.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 175°C for 40–50 minutes to a deep gold, tenting with foil if the top darkens early; the core should reach 88°C. Rest 10 minutes in the pan, then cool on a rack.

source.adaptation: Yeast normalized to 14 g active dry (the source's tsp and gram figures disagree); yeastPct given as instant-yeast equivalent.
